message:
chore: rolling sync of concurrent agent changes

Capturing ongoing concurrent development.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -760,7 +760,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.2.1","title":"Backdrop Internals: Buffer Caching + Zero-Alloc Render Path","description":"## Goal
Ensure Backdrop rendering is **zero-alloc in steady state** by caching and reusing internal buffers.

## Scope
- Backdrop owns `Vec<PackedRgba>` buffers sized to `area.width * area.height`.
- Grow-only policy: reallocate only when capacity is insufficient.
- Never use per-frame `vec![..]` or `push` patterns in the hot path.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit test: capacity does not change across many renders at a stable size.
- Unit test: resize to larger size grows once; resize smaller does not shrink.
- Unit test: buffer is fully written each frame (no stale pixels).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Backdrop steady-state render performs 0 vector growth operations.
- Resize path reallocates at most once per growth event.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:32:46.520865360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:11:27.230027223Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:11:27.229999821Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: zero-alloc buffer caching with grow-only policy. All 9 tests pass: capacity stable across renders, resize larger grows once, resize smaller does not shrink, buffer fully written with no stale pixels.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":517,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Strategy

Backdrop rendering must not allocate every frame.

Implementation idea:
- Backdrop widget owns a `Vec<PackedRgba>` buffer sized to `area.width * area.height`.
- On resize, reallocate only when capacity is insufficient; otherwise reuse.
- Always fill slice in-place.

Key considerations:
- avoid repeated `vec![..]` patterns
- avoid per-cell `push` patterns (must be index-based)

Acceptance:
- [ ] no allocation in steady-state (same area sizes)
- [ ] resize path reallocates at most O(1) per resize event
- [ ] effect render contract accepts `&mut [PackedRgba]` to enable reuse
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:32:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.2.2","title":"Backdrop Legibility: Scrim Policies + Contrast Helpers","description":"## Goal
Provide scrim policies + minimal contrast helpers so text remains readable over animated backdrops by default.

## Scope

1) Scrim policies (cheap + deterministic)
- None
- Solid scrim: `(color, opacity)`
- Vertical fade: top->bottom opacity
- Vignette: edges darker than center

2) Contrast helpers (minimal)
- `luminance(PackedRgba) -> f32`
- `contrast_ratio(fg, bg) -> f32`
- helper to clamp scrim opacity into safe bounds

3) Defaults
- Safe defaults for markdown/text-heavy panels:
  - base fill opaque
  - effect opacity modest (e.g., 0.15–0.35)
  - scrim enabled for text panels (policy decided by the caller/widget)

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for luminance/contrast math and opacity clamping.
- Unit tests for scrim policies (solid/fade/vignette) on tiny buffers.
- Optional property tests: contrast ratio does not drop below a configured floor when using default scrim.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default scrim presets materially improve readability.
- Scrim opacity is bounded (no accidental fully opaque/fully transparent extremes unless explicitly requested).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:32:59.988165019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:54:06.407255787Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T00:54:06.407233605Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["accessibility","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3vbf.29","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":518,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

We want the "cool background effect behind markdown" experience without sacrificing readability.

Backdrop legibility must be:
- predictable
- cheap
- composable

## Scrim Policy Options

A scrim is a semi-opaque overlay composited above the effect and below text.

Policies (selectable):
- None
- FullArea { color, opacity }
- VerticalFade { top_opacity, bottom_opacity } (useful for log UIs)
- UnderTextOnly (future: requires layout knowledge / child widget cooperation)

## Contrast Helpers

We should provide minimal helpers for future use:
- luminance(bg: PackedRgba) -> f32
- contrast_ratio(fg, bg) -> f32
- choose_legible_fg(bg, theme) -> PackedRgba (optional)

Keep this cheap and deterministic.

## Relationship To Existing Work

This overlaps with accessibility / contrast validation work:
- `bd-3vbf.29` (related)

We should avoid duplicating policy in two places; ideally this task implements the practical primitives and bd-3vbf.29 can validate/report.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Backdrop exposes a scrim knob that materially improves readability.
- [ ] Defaults are safe for markdown/text panels.
- [ ] Helpers are documented and tested (unit tests, no heavy deps).
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:33:12Z"},{"id":519,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: implemented scrim policies (uniform/vertical fade/vignette) + contrast helpers + opacity clamping in ftui-extras visual_fx, added unit tests; fixed demo showcase compile issues; cargo fmt/check/clippy all pass.","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:53:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.2.3","title":"Backdrop Composition Helpers: WithBackdrop Wrapper + Ergonomic Builders","description":"## Goal
Make backdrop usage a one-liner at call sites while keeping ownership/state clear and performance predictable.

## Scope
- Provide `WithBackdrop` (or equivalent) that renders:
  1) backdrop
  2) child widget
- Provide ergonomic builders:
  - `Backdrop::new(effect).opacity(...).scrim(...).over(child)`
  - optional presets for safe defaults (subtle/vibrant).

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: render order (backdrop first) and default config values.
- Snapshot/regression tests: wrapping a widget does not change layout/padding semantics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Existing widgets can be wrapped without modifying their internal rendering.
- Defaults are safe/legible and do not surprise users.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:33:23.466983015Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:17:05.061994544Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extras"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":520,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Make backdrop composition effortless and obvious at call sites.

Desired usage shape:

```rust
let ui = WithBackdrop::new(
    Backdrop::new(&mut metaballs).opacity(0.25).scrim(...),
    MarkdownPanel::new(md),
);
ui.render(area, frame);
```

Or builder form:

```rust
backdrop(metaballs).opacity(0.25).scrim_full(theme.bg_overlay, 0.7).over(child)
```

Constraints:
- avoid complicated type signatures if possible
- keep ownership/state story clear (effects are stateful)

Acceptance:
- [ ] minimal boilerplate to layer backdrop + child
- [ ] documentation examples show markdown-over-backdrop
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:33:30Z"},{"id":521,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: Implemented core composition helpers in ftui-extras visual_fx:
- WithBackdrop wrapper (backdrop first, then child)
- Backdrop::render_with and Backdrop::over
Remaining scope: ergonomic builder chaining (opacity/scrim presets) + docs/snapshots if desired.","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:17:05Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-l8x9.2.4","title":"Backdrop Runtime Integration: Use Frame Degradation -> FxQuality","description":"## Goal
Automatically degrade decorative backdrops under load by mapping `frame.buffer.degradation` -> `FxQuality`.

## Scope
- Read `frame.buffer.degradation` during Backdrop render.
- Map via the canonical function from bd-l8x9.1.3.
- Provide an optional override for demos/testing (so we can force Full/Reduced/Off deterministically).

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: mapping is applied every render when no override is set.
- Unit tests: override wins and is deterministic.
- Regression test: `FxQuality::Off` skips expensive effect compute (but still applies base fill and scrim if configured).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Quality is derived from runtime budget unless explicitly overridden.
- Low-budget modes remain safe/legible (base fill + scrim still work).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:33:37.362121946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:28:02.936249375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":522,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Intent

FX should never be the reason the UI feels laggy. When the runtime degrades rendering quality, backdrops should automatically become cheaper.

Implementation notes:
- Backdrop can read `frame.buffer.degradation` (already stored on Buffer).
- Map to `FxQuality` using the explicit mapping defined in bd-l8x9.1.3.
- Effects may additionally degrade based on area size (e.g., terminal 240x80).

Acceptance:
- [ ] At `EssentialOnly`/`Skeleton`, decorative effects are skipped.
- [ ] At reduced budgets, effects use fewer iterations / cheaper math.
- [ ] This policy is documented so callers aren't surprised.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:33:44Z"},{"id":523,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added explicit blocks dependency on bd-l8x9.1.3 so the degradation->FxQuality mapping is defined before wiring runtime degradation into the Backdrop hot path.","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:12:52Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-l8x9.2.4","title":"Backdrop Runtime Integration: Use Frame Degradation -> FxQuality","description":"## Goal
Automatically degrade decorative backdrops under load by mapping `frame.buffer.degradation` -> `FxQuality`.

## Scope
- Read `frame.buffer.degradation` during Backdrop render.
- Map via the canonical function from bd-l8x9.1.3.
- Provide an optional override for demos/testing (so we can force Full/Reduced/Off deterministically).

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: mapping is applied every render when no override is set.
- Unit tests: override wins and is deterministic.
- Regression test: `FxQuality::Off` skips expensive effect compute (but still applies base fill and scrim if configured).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Quality is derived from runtime budget unless explicitly overridden.
- Low-budget modes remain safe/legible (base fill + scrim still work).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:33:37.362121946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T16:56:50.183795265Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":522,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Intent

FX should never be the reason the UI feels laggy. When the runtime degrades rendering quality, backdrops should automatically become cheaper.

Implementation notes:
- Backdrop can read `frame.buffer.degradation` (already stored on Buffer).
- Map to `FxQuality` using the explicit mapping defined in bd-l8x9.1.3.
- Effects may additionally degrade based on area size (e.g., terminal 240x80).

Acceptance:
- [ ] At `EssentialOnly`/`Skeleton`, decorative effects are skipped.
- [ ] At reduced budgets, effects use fewer iterations / cheaper math.
- [ ] This policy is documented so callers aren't surprised.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:33:44Z"},{"id":523,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added explicit blocks dependency on bd-l8x9.1.3 so the degradation->FxQuality mapping is defined before wiring runtime degradation into the Backdrop hot path.","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:12:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.2.5","title":"Backdrop Composition: Stack Multiple FX Layers With One Buffer Apply","description":"Add a library-grade way to stack multiple BackdropFx layers (e.g., gradient + metaballs + noise) while preserving performance: compute/composite into a single bg buffer and apply to the frame once.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stacking multiple effects requires only a single pass of `buf.set` over cells (one final apply to the frame).
- [ ] No per-frame allocations in steady state (buffers reused).
- [ ] Layer ordering semantics are explicit and deterministic (A over B vs B over A is documented and tested).
- [ ] Unit tests cover 2-layer composition equivalence and no-alloc steady-state behavior.

## Extreme Optimization
- [ ] Composition uses tight loops and avoids redundant per-layer per-cell work; measure before/after with representative scenes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:15:59.585083254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:03:37.988257433Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:03:37.988237225Z","close_reason":"Implemented StackedFx compositor for multi-layer BackdropFx composition with single-pass output. Features: BlendMode enum (Over/Additive/Multiply/Screen), FxLayer wrapper, grow-only buffer reuse, 14 unit tests covering composition, blending, and no-alloc behavior.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extras","performance","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":524,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.2.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## User Value

"Trivially composable" should not mean "N full-screen passes per layer".

Users should be able to do:
- subtle gradient base
- metaballs at 0.20 opacity
- plasma at 0.10 opacity
- scrim under text

…without paying O(layers * w*h) buffer writes to the frame.

## Proposed API Shapes (pick one)

A) `BackdropStack` widget
- owns:
  - `Vec<PackedRgba>` main buffer
  - `Vec<PackedRgba>` scratch buffer
- renders:
  - fill base
  - for each layer:
    - effect renders into scratch
    - apply layer opacity -> composite scratch over main
  - apply main to frame once

B) `CompositeFx` implementing `BackdropFx`
- wraps multiple `BackdropFx` and composes them into a caller-provided dst
- still needs scratch (owned by wrapper) but integrates cleanly with existing `Backdrop` widget

C) Tuple impls (advanced)
- implement `BackdropFx` for `(A, B)` / `(A, B, C)` to avoid trait objects
- may still need scratch; keep it explicit

## Performance Rules

- No per-frame allocations (buffers reused).
- No `Box<dyn BackdropFx>` unless we can prove it doesn’t matter (prefer generics).
- Composition must be stable/deterministic.

## Tests To Add

- unit: 2-layer composition equals sequential rendering + Buffer::set semantics
- unit: no-alloc steady-state
- unit: ordering matters and is documented (A over B vs B over A)

## Acceptance

- [ ] stacking multiple effects requires only a single pass of `buf.set` over cells
- [ ] API is ergonomic enough to use in `bd-l8x9.7` (markdown-over-backdrop)
- [ ] layering is deterministic and well-documented","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:16:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.3","title":"Extract Metaballs Into ftui-extras (BackdropFx)","description":"## Goal
Extract metaballs into `ftui-extras` as a reusable `BackdropFx` implementation.

## Scope
- Move core field math + colorization into `crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/metaballs.rs`.
- Provide `MetaballsParams` + a small set of presets that derive colors from `ThemeInputs`.
- Implement quality scaling (`FxQuality`) so reduced budgets stay responsive.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: determinism under fixed inputs; tiny-area safety.
- No-alloc proxy: repeated renders at stable size do not grow internal buffers.
- Integration: used as a backdrop behind a text panel (validated via bd-l8x9.7).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Metaballs is usable as a library effect (not demo-owned code).
- No per-frame allocations required by the effect.","notes":"MetaballsFx module files added under crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/ (no visual_fx.rs wiring yet). Waiting on visual_fx.rs reservations (BronzeGrove/CloudyCove) to export + integrate tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:33:51.868818039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:22:52.801672475Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:22:52.801653369Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extras","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":525,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

Metaballs are currently implemented in:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs` (`MetaballsState`)

They compute an implicit field sum and colorize by weighted hue.

## Goal

Provide a library-grade `MetaballsFx` that can be used as:
- a cell-background backdrop behind real widgets
- (optionally later) a canvas/high-res demo renderer

## Design Requirements

- deterministic output given `(w, h, t, params)`
- no per-frame allocations
- quality scaling: fewer balls / fewer iterations / lower frequency at Reduced/Minimal
- theme-aware palette: do not hardcode demo palettes; use ThemeInputs

## Implementation Notes

- Port the state and update logic, but expose:
  - `MetaballsParams` / presets (e.g., "Aurora", "Lava", "Ocean")
  - `MetaballsFx::step(dt)` or accept absolute time `t`

- Rendering target: fill `out[y*w+x] = PackedRgba` backgrounds.

- Performance: avoid per-pixel sqrt; keep dist^2 math; consider precomputing normalized coords per column/row when beneficial.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Metaballs moved out of demo screen into `ftui-extras`.
- [ ] Backdrop composition works (metaballs behind text panel).
- [ ] Presets exist and look good under at least 2 themes.
- [ ] Reduced/Minimal modes produce a cheaper but still attractive output.

## Tests

- determinism test: fixed seed/params -> stable output hash
- bounds safety: tiny w/h does not panic
- perf smoke: rendering does not allocate in steady state
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.3.1","title":"MetaballsFx: Port Core Field + Colorization (Cell-Space)","description":"## Task
Port the core metaballs field computation to cell-space rendering with explicit sampling and colorization hooks.

## Scope
- Field equation + thresholding for blobs.
- Distance/smoothing parameters normalized by `FxQuality`.
- Output colorization separate from field math (so palettes can be swapped).

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: field continuity, threshold boundaries.
- Determinism test: fixed seed + time yields identical cell values.
- Tiny-area test: 1x1/2x2 buffer does not panic and is stable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Field math produces stable shapes across time.
- No division-by-zero or NaN under degenerate sizes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:19.392928744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:06:55.386524496Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:06:55.386504979Z","close_reason":"Added unit tests for field monotonicity and threshold boundaries in MetaballsFx","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":526,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Notes:
- Source reference: `MetaballsState::render()` in `visual_effects.rs`.
- Replace `(px,py)` loop over painter space with `(x,y)` loop over cells.
- Keep threshold/glow thresholds but parameterize them.
- Output color should include alpha (optionally <255) so caller can tune opacity via composition.

Acceptance:
- [ ] No allocations during render (no Vec push, no per-pixel Vec).
- [ ] Output uses ThemeInputs-driven hues/accents instead of hardcoded HSV palettes.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:25Z"},{"id":527,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started. visual_fx.rs still reserved by BronzeGrove (bd-l8x9.2.2); waiting for release before code changes. Will port MetaballsState::render into BackdropFx cell-space with palette hooks and tests.","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:18:23Z"}]}
